system.description: Disposable air filters for masks, helmets, or breathing gear.
system.notes: Cost: 5 credits. Carry spare filters when traveling in smoke, dust, or tainted air.

system.description: A large, high-quality adventuring pack for general travel and field gear.
system.notes: Cost: 100-200 credits.

system.description: A small, high-quality pack for light travel and personal gear.
system.notes: Cost: 40-100 credits.

system.description: A simple bedroll for field camps and overnight travel.
system.notes: Cost: 30 credits.

system.description: A heavy blanket for cold-weather travel or rough field camps.
system.notes: Cost: 20 credits.

system.description: A standard field canteen for carrying drinking water.
system.notes: Cost: 20 credits.

system.description: A basic navigation compass for overland travel.
system.notes: Cost: 50-150 credits.

system.description: A tough carry bag for extra gear, clothing, or supplies.
system.notes: Cost: 25-80 credits.

system.description: A face mask that protects against smoke, dust, and many airborne threats.
system.notes: Human-size cost: 50-80 credits. Larger sizes cost 80-120 credits.

system.description: A grappling hook with 100 feet of line for climbing, crossing gaps, or hauling gear.
system.notes: Cost: 80 credits.

system.description: Twenty feet of rope for climbing, tying down gear, or field work.
system.notes: Cost: 15 credits per 20 feet.

system.description: A travel sleeping bag for wilderness camps and rough shelter.
system.notes: Cost: 110-160 credits.

system.description: A basic field radio used for short-range military or adventuring communication.
system.notes: Range: 3 miles. S.D.C.: 30. Cost: 150 credits.

system.description: A compact clipped communicator for personal field use.
system.notes: Range: 5 miles. S.D.C.: 10. Cost: 1,500 credits, or 3,500 credits for a 1 M.D.C. version.

system.description: A walkie-talkie-sized field communicator with better range and durability.
system.notes: Range: 10 miles. S.D.C.: 30. Cost: 3,000 credits, or 6,000 credits for a 4 M.D.C. version.

system.description: A protective helmet with built-in two-way radio and automatic visor adjustment.
system.notes: Range: 5 or 10 miles. M.D.C.: 30 or 50 for the helmet. Cost: 5,500 or 10,000 credits.

system.description: A hand computer with scanner, rangefinder, and standard field programs.
system.notes: Cost: 2,500 credits. Use it for calculations, word processing, measurements, and data transfer in the field.

system.description: A backpack radio transmitter and receiver for long-range field communication.
system.notes: Range: 60 miles in cities or cluttered areas, 150 miles in the wilderness. Cost: 600 credits.

system.description: A portable translation unit for spoken languages and recorded conversation.
system.notes: Translates up to three voices. Cost: 9,600 credits.

system.description: A hardened backpack radio with long-range military transmission capability.
system.notes: Range: 300 miles in cluttered areas or 500 miles in the wilderness. Cost: 6,000 credits, or 16,000 credits for a 15 M.D.C. version.

system.description: A handheld video-and-radio communicator for mobile field contact.
system.notes: Range: 10 miles, or double in open areas. Cost: 10,000 credits.

system.description: A portable vital-sign monitor that records a patient?s immediate condition.
system.notes: Cost: 2,500 credits.

system.description: A standard first-aid kit with bandages, wipes, tape, aspirin, and simple field tools.
system.notes: Cost: 100 credits.

system.description: A quick, low-pain injector for administering medicines and shots.
system.notes: Cost: 200 credits, drugs not included.

system.description: A medical unit that releases microscopic surgical robots to repair internal injury.
system.notes: Equal to a surgical skill of 75% for internal repair work. Four uses per unit. Cost: 42,000 credits.

name: IRMSS Internal Robot Medical Surgeon System

system.description: A compact robot medical kit that cleans, disinfects, and closes wounds.
system.notes: Equal to a paramedic suturing skill of 90%. Cost: 24,000 credits.

system.description: A protein-rich salve used on burns, cuts, and rashes to improve recovery.
system.notes: Doubles normal healing on treated wounds. Cost: 100 credits per tube.

system.description: A binocular system with infrared adjustments, cross-hair indicators, and range readout.
system.notes: Range: 2 miles. Cost: 1,200 credits.

system.description: A protective helmet with targeting sight, infrared optics, telescopic monocular, and thermal imaging.
system.notes: Targeting sight and thermal imager grant +1 to strike when engaged. Cost: 2,800-3,400 credits.

system.description: Passive nightvision goggles that amplify ambient light.
system.notes: Range: 1,600 feet. Cost: 1,800 credits.

system.description: A headband-style optics package for close technical work and research.
system.notes: Includes infrared, ultraviolet, magnification, night sight, and color filters. Cost: 800-1,200 credits.

system.description: A compact monocular night viewer for concealed or portable observation.
system.notes: Range: 800 feet. Cost: 800-1,000 credits.

system.description: An optical heat sensor that lets the user see warm targets through darkness, shadow, and smoke.
system.notes: Range: 1,600 feet. Cost: about 2,000 credits.

system.description: A portable radar unit able to track many airborne targets at once.
system.notes: Tracks up to 72 images. Range: 5 miles. Cost: 2,500 credits.

system.description: A portable movement sensor that detects minute shifts in the air.
system.notes: Range: 60 feet. Cost: 400 credits.

system.description: A handheld device that scrambles outgoing radio signals.
system.notes: Use it to reduce the chance of interception. Cost: 300 credits.

system.description: A small tracking bug that can be attached to gear, vehicles, or a target?s belongings.
system.notes: Transmission range: 8 miles. Battery life: 72 hours. Cost: 140 credits.

system.description: A compact microphone for short-range listening and transmission.
system.notes: Picks up sound up to 14 feet away and broadcasts up to 300 feet. Cost: 250 credits.
